channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Bard College's president to retire after scrutiny of relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: The longtime president of Bard College in New York has announced his retirement, months after it was revealed that he had a much deeper relationship than was previously known with Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded:
The longtime president of Bard College in New York has announced his retirement, months after it was revealed that he had a much deeper relationship than was previously known with Jeffrey Epstein.

(Image credit: Philip Kamrass)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the 1st woman to train its winner
item.description: Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.
item.content:encoded:
Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

(Image credit: Jeff Roberson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Comedian Sheng Wang on finding joy in the mundane
item.description: In his new Netflix special, comedian Sheng Wang elevates mundane moments - like picking a toothbrush - into punchlines. NPR's Emily Feng talks to the comedian about where he finds joy.
item.content:encoded: In his new Netflix special, comedian Sheng Wang elevates mundane moments - like picking a toothbrush - into punchlines. NPR's Emily Feng talks to the comedian about where he finds joy.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Academy announces major overhaul to rules
item.description: The new rules focus on areas such as AI protections for writers and actors and expanded eligibility for international films.
item.content:encoded:
The new rules focus on areas such as AI protections for writers and actors and expanded eligibility for international films.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: How the war in Iran is affecting jet fuel prices and flights
item.description: Spirit Airlines ceased operation overnight. This comes as jet fuel prices - which have soared since the start of the war on Iran - have led to of thousands of flight cuts in Europe and in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: Spirit Airlines ceased operation overnight. This comes as jet fuel prices - which have soared since the start of the war on Iran - have led to of thousands of flight cuts in Europe and in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: How this Boston Public Library 'Tell-A-Booth' creates a comforting space for visitors
item.description: A phone booth has been installed at the Boston Public Library where visitors can leave messages. NPR's Scott Simon talks to Meredith Goldstein from the Boston Globe about her project.
item.content:encoded: A phone booth has been installed at the Boston Public Library where visitors can leave messages. NPR's Scott Simon talks to Meredith Goldstein from the Boston Globe about her project.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: A Louisiana man exonerated for a crime was elected court clerk. His role was eliminated
item.description: A man who spent 28 years in prison for a crime he did not commit was elected as clerk of court in New Orleans. Then, state lawmakers eliminated his position.
item.content:encoded: A man who spent 28 years in prison for a crime he did not commit was elected as clerk of court in New Orleans. Then, state lawmakers eliminated his position.
item.dc:creator: Eve Abrams

item.title: The 'Palisadian-Post' is making a comeback under new local ownership
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon speaks with Laura and Tim Schneider, who are relaunching the Palisadian-Post after it closed due to the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon speaks with Laura and Tim Schneider, who are relaunching the Palisadian-Post after it closed due to the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations after escalating financial struggles
item.description: The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.
item.content:encoded:
The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Air Force says former Qatari 747 will be ready to fly as Air Force One this summer
item.description: The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.

(Image credit: Matt Rourke)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
